title: veronica fruticulosa subsp. fruticans
intro: Veronica fruticulosa subsp. fruticans, also known as the shrubby speedwell, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The shrubby speedwell has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions, and has been shown to have antioxidant and immune-boosting properties. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its potential health benefits.
